title: Service Definition Schema v1.0.5 - June 24, 2026
description: Complete canonical schema for Service Envelope YAML definitions. Aligns with SERVICE_DEFINITION_RULES.yaml. v1.0.5 adds OTP-era bearer auth assumptions and default lifecycle email template fallback fields.

id.description: Service identifier (free-form; UI may provide custom format)

type.description: Service type identifier (e.g., courseEnrollment, roomRental, requestTOR)

description.description: Detailed description of what the service does

initiator.description: OPTIONAL: Role/type of user who can initiate (e.g., Student, Faculty, Administrator) - admin metadata only

envelopes.description: 6-envelope pipeline configuration. Only REQUEST is mandatory; all others are optional (required: false). Optional envelopes can be empty {}.

request.description: REQUEST ENVELOPE (ALWAYS REQUIRED) - Collect user parameters

approval.description: APPROVAL ENVELOPE (optional) - Authorization workflow with multiple approvers

processing.description: PROCESSING ENVELOPE (optional) - Sequential API task execution

delivery.description: DELIVERY ENVELOPE (optional) - Send documents via email/physical_mail/pickup

requestEnvelope.description: REQUEST ENVELOPE - Validates incoming user parameters. ALWAYS REQUIRED. Must have required: true for consistency with other envelopes.

required.description: REQUEST envelope must be true (required for consistency; REQUEST is always required)

parameters.description: Parameter schema: keys are parameter names, values define type/constraints

required.description: Is this parameter required from user?

min.description: Number/Date: minimum value or earliest date

max.description: Number/Date: maximum value or latest date

default.description: Default value if user doesn't provide

defaultEmailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Optional request-start default template before generic fallback

defaultEmailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Optional request-end default template before generic fallback

approvalEnvelope.description: APPROVAL ENVELOPE (optional) - Multi-person authorization workflow with rule types: all_must_approve, any_one, specific_approver, complex

approvalRules.description: Approval decision rules (required if required: true)

type.description: Rule logic: all_must_approve=AND, any_one=OR, specific_approver=single person, complex=mixed

requiredApprovers.description: For all_must_approve/complex: all approvers must approve (email addresses)

specificApprover.description: For specific_approver: only this person can approve (email address)

atLeastOneOf.description: For complex: at least one approver from this group must approve (email addresses)

threshold.description: For any_one: how many approvals needed before continuing

emailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when approval envelope begins (e.g., SERV-999-approval-start)

defaultEmailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Default approval-start template used when no override is configured

emailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when approval envelope completes (e.g., SERV-999-approval-end)

defaultEmailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Default approval-end template used when no override is configured

emailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when approval is DENIED and request is CANCELLED (e.g., SERV-999-request-cancelled)

defaultEmailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Default approval-cancel template used before service/global generic fallback

paymentEnvelope.description: PAYMENT ENVELOPE (optional) - Financial transaction processing via MAYA, Stripe, PayPal, or GCash

charges.description: Charges to collect (required if required: true). Each charge: amount × quantity = item total

expiryDays.description: Days before payment window expires and request is cancelled

emailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when payment is required (e.g., SERV-999-payment-start, includes {{paymentLink}})

defaultEmailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Default payment-start template used when no override is configured

emailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when payment is received (e.g., SERV-999-payment-end)

defaultEmailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Default payment-end receipt template used before generic fallback

emailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when payment expires and request is CANCELLED (e.g., SERV-999-request-cancelled)

defaultEmailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Default payment-cancel template used before service/global generic fallback

processingEnvelope.description: PROCESSING ENVELOPE (optional) - Sequential API task execution with HTTP methods (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH)

tasks.description: API tasks to execute sequentially (required if required: true)

retries.description: Number of retry attempts on failure

successCodes.description: HTTP status codes that indicate success (e.g., [200, 201])

stopOnFailure.description: If true: stop pipeline on first task failure. If false: skip failed tasks, continue

emailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when processing begins (e.g., SERV-999-processing-start)

defaultEmailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Default processing-start template used when no override is configured

emailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when processing completes (e.g., SERV-999-processing-end)

defaultEmailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Default processing-end template used when no override is configured

emailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when task fails and request is CANCELLED (e.g., SERV-999-request-cancelled)

defaultEmailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Default processing-cancel template used before service/global generic fallback

deliveryEnvelope.description: DELIVERY ENVELOPE (optional, NEW) - Send documents via email, physical mail, or in-person pickup. Method and details submitted separately via POST /api/delivery/{requestId}/details

deliveryMethods.description: Delivery method configurations (required if required: true)

email.description: EMAIL METHOD - Instant delivery via email with attachments

recipient.description: Recipient field name (e.g., {{email}} from request parameters)

attachmentUrls.description: URLs of documents to attach (supports {{parameterName}} substitution)

physical_mail.description: PHYSICAL_MAIL METHOD - Courier delivery (LBC, JNT, DHL, etc.)

costPercentage.description: Percentage to add to total payment if selected

requiresApprovalBeforeShip.description: Admin must approve before courier pickup

fields.description: Form fields to collect from user (e.g., mailingAddress)

pickup.description: PICKUP METHOD - In-person pickup at designated location

pickupDeadlineDays.description: Days to hold document before discarding

notificationRequired.description: Send email when ready for pickup

emailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when delivery envelope begins (e.g., SERV-999-delivery-start)

defaultEmailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Default delivery-start template used when no override is configured

emailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when delivery completes (e.g., SERV-999-delivery-end)

defaultEmailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Default delivery-end template used when no override is configured

defaultEmailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Default delivery-cancel template used before service/global generic fallback

feedbackEnvelope.description: FEEDBACK ENVELOPE (optional, NEW) - Post-completion feedback collection with auto-close and expiry

autoCloseAfterHours.description: Hours before request auto-closes (0 or omit = no auto-close). Typically 24 for quick surveys

expiryDays.description: Days before feedback link expires and cannot be submitted

reminderDaysBefore.description: Days before expiry to send reminder (only if notificationRequired: true)

emailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when feedback envelope begins (e.g., SERV-999-feedback-start, includes {{feedbackLink}})

defaultEmailTemplateStartEnvelope.description: Default feedback-start template used when no override is configured

emailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Email template ID sent when feedback is submitted (e.g., SERV-999-feedback-end)

defaultEmailTemplateEndEnvelope.description: Default feedback-end template used when no override is configured

defaultEmailTemplateCancelEnvelope.description: Default feedback-cancel template used before service/global generic fallback
